description: Figure 7 presents overlaid X-ray diffraction patterns of two intercritically annealed low-density medium Mn steels: M1-IA (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C, black curve) and M2-IA (Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C, red curve), both processed at 700°C for 1 h and water quenched. The patterns span 2θ from 40° to 110° and display peaks corresponding to body-centered-cubic α-ferrite (α 110, α 200, α 211, α 220) and face-centered-cubic austenite (γ 111, γ 200, γ 220, γ 311, γ 222). The M1-IA sample exhibits substantially stronger austenite reflections and a quantitative phase fraction of fγ = 45.6%, while the M2-IA sample shows a dominant α 110 peak and a much lower retained austenite fraction of fγ = 19.6%. This direct comparison confirms that 1 wt.% Si addition drastically reduces austenite retention after intercritical annealing, which the caption and text attribute to the stabilization of coarse band-type δ-ferrite in M2-IA and altered Mn partitioning, thereby governing austenite stability and the resulting mechanical properties.
